original: The United States Environmental Protection Agency announces its intent to buy out and evacuate the dioxin-contaminated community of Times Beach, Missouri
American speed skaterEric Heidenwins Olympic 10,000m in world record 14:28.13; sweeps all 5 speed skating events at Lake Placid; only athlete to win 5 gold medals at one Winter Games
Lydia Wideman of Finland becomes first female Olympic cross-country skiing champion; wins inaugural 10k event in Olso; Finnish medal sweep with minors to Mirja Hietamies and Siiri Rantanen
Michael Frayn's "Noises Off" premieres in London
Nelli Cooman sets world record for 60m indoor (7.00 sec) at European Indoor Championships in Madrid, Spain
Supernova 1987A in LMC 1st seen; 1st naked-eye supernova since 1604
Sky Disc of Nebra, world's oldest map of the stars at 3,600 years old, recovered in police sting operation in Basal, Switzerland after being illegally excavated in Germany
Discovery of oldest Australian rock art, a 17,300-year-old painting of a kangaroo in Kimberley region, Western Australia, published in "Nature Human Behaviour"
US Marines raise the flag of the United States on top of Mt. Suribachi, Iwo Jima. Pulitzer Prize-winning photo by Joe Rosenthal later becomes iconic, inspiring the Marine Corps War Memorial sculpture
Walt Disney's animated movie "Pinocchio" released
Soviet cross-country skierNikolay Zimyatovwins his 3rd gold medal of the Lake Placid Winter Olympics when he takes out the 50k event; also wins 30k and relay gold
First mass inoculation against polio with theJonas Salkvaccine takes place at Arsenal Elementary School in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
Fiordland National Park in New Zealand created, home to the Milford Track and at 4,600 square miles (12,000 square km), one of the world's largest
Sri Lanka becomes first Asian cricket team to clinch a Test series in South Africa chasing down 197 in the 2nd Test in Port Elizabeth; Kusal Mendis 84no, Oshada Fernando 75no
Johannes Gutenbergprints his first Bible [estimated date]
Argentine soccer starLionel Messiscores the 50th hat-trick of his career as Barcelona beats Sevilla, 4-2 in the Spanish La Liga
Emma Willard, American women's rights activist and educator who founded the 1st school for women's higher education in the US, born in Berlin, Connecticut (d. 1870)
Didier Queloz, Swiss astronomer (discovered 1st exoplanet), 2019 Nobel Prize in Physics, born in Switzerland
Plutonium first produced and isolated by American chemist Glenn T. Seaborg at Berkeley
Following his Nordic combined gold medal in St. Moritz (1948), Heikki Hasu takes his 2nd Olympic gold as part of Finland’s 4 x 10k cross country relay team at the Olso Winter Games
Frank Peterson Jr named 1st black general in Marine Corps
Joe Louisretains his world heavyweight boxing title by knocking out Nathan Mann in round 3 at Madison Square Garden, New York; second defense by Louis
Baron von Steuben joins Continental Army at Valley Forge
Wilt Chamberlainbecomes the first NBA player to score 25,000 points
Major Walter Clopton Wingfield patents a game he calls "sphairistike," now known as lawn tennis
Boston Bruins begin 13 NHL game winning streak
EngineerJames Watt's patent for a rotary motion for the steam engine (his sun-and-planet gear) is granted
Tommy Burnsbeats Marvin Hart in 20 for heavyweight boxing title
Third appearance by the Beatles on "TheEd SullivanShow", featuring performances of three songs taped on February 9, prior to their live television debut
GoaltenderPatrick Roymakes his NHL debut for the Montreal Canadiens
Agnes Arber, English biologist and philosopher (Mind & the Eye, 1st woman botanist elected to the Royal Society), born in London, England (d. 1960)
Ed "Too Tall" Jones, American NFL defensive end (Pro Bowl, All Pro 1981-83; Super Bowl 1977; Dallas Cowboys), born in Jackson, Tennessee
Dana Katherine Scully, American fictional character (The X-Files), born in Annapolis, Maryland
Francisco Vázquez de Coronado's expedition sets off from Mexico in search of the Seven Cities of Gold (Cibola)
NC is 1st NCAA basketball team to win 1,500 games
Erich Kästner, German writer poet and screenwriter (Emil and the Detectives ), born in Dresden, Germany (d. 1974)
Ray Brown, American Baseball HOF pitcher (NgL World Series 1943, 44; Triple Crown 1938; Homestead Grays), born in Alger, Ohio (d. 1965)
Majel Barrett, American actress and producer (Star Trek), and the widow of Gene Roddenberry, born in Cleveland, Ohio (d. 2008)
Johnny Winter [John Dawson], American blues guitarist (Silver Train), born in Leland, Mississippi (d. 2014)
Doug Moench, American comic book writer known for his work on Batman and "Master of Kung Fu", born in Chicago, Illinois
Sylvie Guillem, French ballerina (Royal Ballet, 1989-2015), born in Paris, France
Niecy Nash [Carol Denise Ensley], American actress, comedienne, and TV host (When They See Us), born in Compton, California
Study suggests earliest European Palaeolithic artwork in Spain made by Neanderthals not humans in "Science Journal"
Fire in the Hofburg in Vienna, Emperor Leopold I rescued
Humane Society of Massachusetts incorporates to establish life-saving stations for distressed mariners
College of Apothecaries organized in Philadephia; 1st US pharmacy college
Aluminum manufacturing process developed
Tootsie Roll introduced by Leo Hirshfield
United States acquires control of the Panama Canal Zone for $10 million
Groundbreaking TV documentary series "Civilisation" presented by art historian Kenneth Clark premieres on BBC2 in the UK
Cricketer Andy Flower scores 115* on ODI debut as Zimbabwe lose to Sri Lanka
Dutch princeWilliam IIIproclaimed King of England
Lou Theszbeats Everett Marshall in St Louis, to win the National Wrestling Association World Heavyweight C'ship for a 2nd time
US GeneralEisenhoweropens drive to raise $170M in aid for European Jews
Matthias Corvinus, King of Hungary and Croatia (1458-90) who created the Bibliotheca Corviniana, born in Kolozsvár, Kingdom of Hungary (d. 1490)

John Sutter, German-Swiss-American colonist who established Sutter's Fort in what is now Sacramento and where gold was first discovered in California, born in Kandern, Margraviate of Baden, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1880)
Kazimir Malevich, Ukrainian painter and art theorist, born in Kyiv, Russian Empire(d. 1935)
Musidora [Jeanne Roques], French actress and director (Les vampires, Judex), born in Paris, France (d. 1957)
George Abel, Canadian ice hockey player (Olympic gold 1952), born in Melville, Saskatchewan (d. 1996)
Vasily Grigoryevich Lazarev, Soviet Russian cosmonaut (Soyuz 12, Soyuz 18A), born in Siberia, USSR (d. 1990)
